skill.name: pitfalls-express-api
skill.description: Express API conventions and storage patterns. Use when building REST APIs, defining routes, or implementing storage interfaces. Triggers on: Express, router, API route, status code, storage interface.
skill.summary: Guides consistent Express API routes, status codes, validation, storage interfaces, and job cleanup.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptographic algorithm, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, REST route examples, and API status-code wording. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or executable command path was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Findings
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines are documentation examples for Express route structure, storage interfaces, and background jobs, not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks open and close fenced TypeScript documentation blocks. No shell command, Ruby command substitution, or executable wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Findings
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged ordinary API and status-code text as weak cryptography indicators. The skill discusses REST API conventions and HTTP status codes, and it does not define or call any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive API guidance and status code tables. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Static Findings
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged REST route examples as system reconnaissance. The referenced lines show HTTP methods and route names for API design, not host, network, user, or process enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static documentation examples for Express routes, status codes, and interval cleanup. They do not gather system information or instruct the model to inspect the host environment.

content.value_statement: Express APIs often drift in route shape, status codes, validation, and storage boundaries. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a compact checklist for consistent REST API work.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Express route structures against common REST conventions.
Maps API operations to expected success and error status codes.
Prompts validation before storage or business logic runs.
Recommends a storage interface abstraction for database and memory backends.
Highlights cleanup patterns for background intervals and shutdown handling.
limitations: Does not provide a full Express application scaffold.
Does not replace security review for authentication or authorization.
Does not cover every HTTP method or advanced routing pattern.
Does not validate code automatically without the model inspecting project files.

use_cases.title: Plan a New REST Resource
use_cases.description: Define public and admin routes with expected status codes before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Check whether Express handlers validate input, return correct responses, and keep storage concerns isolated.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Backends
use_cases.description: Use a shared storage interface so tests can run against memory storage while production uses a database.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Express route for REST naming, validation placement, and status code choice. List only the changes needed.

prompt_templates.title: Design Resource Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the Express routes for this resource using public read routes and protected admin write routes. Include expected success and error status codes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Storage Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these storage calls and propose an interface that supports database and memory implementations without changing route handlers.

prompt_templates.title: Audit API Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across routing, validation, storage, and jobs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Express API module for route structure, status codes, validation flow, storage abstraction, and background job cleanup. Prioritize risks and fixes.

output_examples.input: A create endpoint returns 200 and writes request body directly to storage.
output: Use 201 for a successful create operation.
Validate and normalize the request body before storage writes.
Keep storage access behind an interface so route handlers stay thin.

output_examples.input: A delete endpoint returns a response body after successful deletion.
output: Return 204 when deletion succeeds and no body is needed.
Return 404 when the resource does not exist.
Keep the route behavior consistent with the status-code table.

output_examples.input: A background scan runs on an interval and can overlap with itself.
output: Track whether the job is already running.
Skip the next run when the previous run has not finished.
Clear intervals during shutdown so the process exits cleanly.

best_practices: Validate request bodies before calling storage or domain logic.
Keep route handlers aligned with predictable HTTP status codes.
Use storage interfaces to separate API behavior from backend choices.
anti_patterns: Writing unvalidated request bodies directly into storage.
Returning 200 for every successful or failed API operation.
Mixing database-specific code throughout route handlers.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps review and design Express REST APIs with consistent routes, status codes, validation, storage boundaries, and job cleanup.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete Express server?
faq.answer: No. It provides conventions and review guidance that can be applied to an existing or planned Express API.

faq.question: Can it review security controls?
faq.answer: It can remind the model to check validation and protected admin routes, but it is not a full security audit skill.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use TypeScript-style interfaces and Express handler patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with testing?
faq.answer: Yes. Its storage interface pattern supports memory implementations that are useful for tests.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
